question_text: The graph below shows the polynomial y = x² − 5x + 6 over its real-root range. How many real zeroes does the polynomial have?

answer_key.explanation: The parabola crosses the x-axis at two distinct points, so the polynomial has two real zeroes.
answer_key.related_concept: Geometrical meaning of the zeroes of a polynomial

visual.caption: Quadratic curve crossing the x-axis.
visual.altText: Upward parabola y equals x squared minus five x plus six plotted on a Cartesian plane from x equals zero to x equals five. The vertical axis ranges from about minus one to four.
